content: We can reframe zero-shot prompting for LLMs as a built-in ICM-like curiosity mechanism, where the LLM’s intrinsic reward for generating coherent out-of-distribution responses to unseen prompts emerges from its pre-training instead of being explicitly engineered. This reframing suggests that pre-trained LLMs already encode an implicit curiosity drive that can be activated via zero-shot prompting, eliminating the need for separate reward fine-tuning for open-ended exploration tasks.
